title: **Local Initiative Aims to Support Veterans Amid Rising Suicide Rates**
excerpt:
**Local Initiative Aims to Support Veterans Amid Rising Suicide Rates**

Rochdale, UK – A community initiative, Op Check In, has been launched to address the alarming statistic that five veterans die ...
content_html:
**Local Initiative Aims to Support Veterans Amid Rising Suicide Rates**

Rochdale, UK – A community initiative, Op Check In, has been launched to address the alarming statistic that five veterans die by suicide each week in the UK. The organisation aims to break the silence surrounding mental health issues within the veteran community and provide essential support to those in need.

The initiative encourages veterans who may be struggling, as well as their families and friends, to reach out for help. By fostering open conversations and offering assistance, Op Check In seeks to reduce the number of veterans facing mental health challenges and ultimately save lives.

Community members are urged to spread the word and support those who may be fighting their battles in silence.

Community members have responded with various recommendations, with one resident, Rachael Davies, suggesting PBS Joinery, led by Paul, who is based in Norden. Another recommendation came from Pete McNally, who endorsed a service provided by an individual named Courtney.

Residents are encouraged to share their experiences and recommendations to assist Teale in finding a suitable tradesperson.

Customers can walk in or message the barbershop to book an appointment. The initiative aims to support those seeking a fresh haircut without the cost. Noble Barbers & Beard Specialists is located in Rochdale.

title: **Local Residents Express Concern Over Fireworks Disturbance**
excerpt:
**Local Residents Express Concern Over Fireworks Disturbance**

Residents in Rochdale have voiced their frustrations regarding the ongoing use of fireworks in the area, particularly during late-night ...
content_html:
**Local Residents Express Concern Over Fireworks Disturbance**

Residents in Rochdale have voiced their frustrations regarding the ongoing use of fireworks in the area, particularly during late-night hours. Paul Thomas Heywood took to social media to express his annoyance, highlighting the distress caused to his dog despite efforts to create a calming environment with music and a comforter.

Another community member, Cathy Rudderforth, echoed these sentiments, stating that her dog experiences severe anxiety due to the noise, fearing it could lead to a serious health issue.

Questions have also been raised about the source of the fireworks, with resident Shale Collins seeking information on where they are being set off. As concerns grow, residents are calling for a more considerate approach to fireworks use in the community.

content_html:
**Residents Express Concerns Over Anti-Social Behaviour in Rochdale**

Rochdale residents are voicing their frustrations regarding a series of disturbances occurring on Dellar Street, near Edenfield Road. Sarah Howarth, a local resident, reported that her parents' house has been subjected to repeated incidents of anti-social behaviour, escalating from door kicking to individuals jumping on parked cars.

Community members have reacted strongly to the situation, with calls for police intervention and suggestions to engage local school authorities, particularly after reports of similar disruptive behaviour linked to students from Oulder Hill School.

As the community seeks solutions, residents are encouraged to report any incidents to local law enforcement to help address the issue effectively.

Residents looking for similar services are encouraged to connect with the suggested tradespeople based on community feedback.

title: **Plans for Major Transformation of Iconic Rochdale Mosque**

Rochdale is set to witness significant changes as plans for a new mosque in the town centre are unveiled. The proposed project aims to tra...
content_html:
**Plans for Major Transformation of Iconic Rochdale Mosque**

Rochdale is set to witness significant changes as plans for a new mosque in the town centre are unveiled. The proposed project aims to transform one of the area’s most recognised religious landmarks into a modern facility, described as a ‘legacy project’ for the community.

Details about the design and funding of the new mosque have yet to be fully disclosed, but local sources indicate that the initiative has garnered support within the community.

As the conversation surrounding the project unfolds, it has sparked various reactions from residents, with discussions focusing on the balance between new developments and existing places of worship.

Further updates on the project are expected as plans progress.

title: **Concerns Raised Over Mosque Construction and Church Closures in Rochdale**

Residents in Rochdale have expressed mixed feelings regarding the construction of new mosques in the area, with some voici...
content_html:
**Concerns Raised Over Mosque Construction and Church Closures in Rochdale**

Residents in Rochdale have expressed mixed feelings regarding the construction of new mosques in the area, with some voicing concerns over the closure and demolition of local churches. One community member highlighted that churches were historically funded by taxpayer money and urged those who wish to preserve these places of worship to engage actively by attending services and contributing financially. The discussion reflects broader community sentiments about the evolving religious landscape in Rochdale.

In her post, she clarified that the mosque is already established, is not funded by taxpayers, and is unrelated to concerns about farmers, old mill sites, housing, or local retail. Cunnane's remarks come amid ongoing discussions within the community, emphasising the importance of informed dialogue.

**Burst Water Main Causes Disruption on Shawfield Lane**

Rochdale, UK - A burst water main on Shawfield Lane has led to disruptions in the area, with reports indicating that residents in the vicinity are experiencing a lack of water supply.

Local resident Rachael Pattie reported the incident shortly after it occurred, highlighting that repairs had only recently been completed on the same section of road earlier that afternoon. Another resident, Christine McHugh, confirmed that several homes in the nearby 7 Acres area are affected by the outage.

Residents are urged to check for updates and remain informed as local authorities work to resolve the issue. Further details on the repair timeline and service restoration are yet to be announced.

title: Arrests after late-night burglary in Heywood
excerpt: Police made two arrests after reports of a late-night burglary near York Street, Heywood.
content_html: Greater Manchester Police confirmed two suspects were detained after officers responded to reports of a break-in near York Street just after midnight. No injuries were reported and enquiries are ongoing.
quotes: Saw flashing lights at around 12:15am – brilliant response time.
Glad no one was hurt. Thank you GMP.

title: Rochdale town centre roadworks: weekend closures announced
excerpt: Temporary closures will be in place this weekend to resurface parts of Yorkshire Street.
content_html: Rochdale Borough Council says diversions will be signposted and buses will continue to run. Drivers are advised to allow extra travel time.
quotes: About time those potholes were fixed!
Please keep access open for businesses.

title: Littleborough school fete raises £4,200 for new library
excerpt: Parents and pupils celebrated a successful summer fete supporting the library project.
content_html: Headteacher Ms. Khan said the funds will go towards books and reading spaces open to the community after school hours.
quotes: The kids can’t wait for the new reading nook!

title: Appeal: witnesses after collision in Milnrow
excerpt: Police are appealing for dashcam footage after a collision on Newhey Road, Milnrow.
content_html: Any witnesses are asked to contact 101 quoting log 2178 of today’s date.
quotes: I drove past just after it happened.

title: Councillors back motion to expand town centre CCTV
excerpt: The council supported a plan to extend CCTV coverage across key town centre spots.
content_html: The motion passed 27-14. Installation is expected later this year, pending procurement.
quotes: Good step for safety if used responsibly.
